info.title: Quotes
info.x-hubspot-api-use-case: Create a contract proposal for a customer who is interested in signing up for one of your annual SEO auditing service packages.
info.x-hubspot-introduction: Use the quotes API to create and manage sales quotes for sharing pricing information with potential buyers.

parameters.name: archived
parameters.description: Whether to return only results that have been archived

post.description: Returns a batch of quotes matching the provided internal IDs or unique property values, with partial success indicated by a 207 response.

get.description: Read an Object identified by `{quoteId}`. `{quoteId}` refers to the internal object ID by default, or optionally any unique property value as specified by the `idProperty` query param.  Control what is returned via the `properties` query param.

parameters.name: quoteId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the quote to retrieve.

parameters.name: properties
parameters.description: A comma separated list of the properties to be returned in the response. If any of the specified properties are not present on the requested object(s), they will be ignored

parameters.name: propertiesWithHistory
parameters.description: A comma separated list of the properties to be returned along with their history of previous values. If any of the specified properties are not present on the requested object(s), they will be ignored

parameters.name: associations
parameters.description: A comma separated list of object types to retrieve associated IDs for. If any of the specified associations do not exist, they will be ignored

parameters.name: archived
parameters.description: Whether to return only results that have been archived

parameters.name: idProperty
parameters.description: The name of a property whose values are unique for this object type

delete.description: Move an Object identified by `{quoteId}` to the recycling bin.

parameters.name: quoteId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the quote to archive.

patch.description: Perform a partial update of an Object identified by `{quoteId}`. `{quoteId}` refers to the internal object ID by default, or optionally any unique property value as specified by the `idProperty` query param. Provided property values will be overwritten. Read-only and non-existent properties will be ignored. Properties values can be cleared by passing an empty string.

parameters.name: quoteId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the quote to update.

parameters.name: idProperty
parameters.description: The name of a property whose values are unique for this object type

post.summary: Archive a batch of quotes by ID
post.description: Archives the specified quotes by ID. Returns no content on success.
Returns a collection of newly created quote objects, including their assigned IDs and submitted property values. A 207 response indicates partial success with error details for failed items.
Returns the updated quote objects reflecting applied property changes. A 207 response indicates partial success with error details for any quotes that could not be updated.

get.description: Read a page of quotes. Control what is returned via the `properties` query param.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: The maximum number of results to display per page

parameters.name: after
parameters.description: The paging cursor token of the last successfully read resource will be returned as the `paging.next.after` JSON property of a paged response containing more results

parameters.name: properties
parameters.description: A comma separated list of the properties to be returned in the response. If any of the specified properties are not present on the requested object(s), they will be ignored

parameters.name: propertiesWithHistory
parameters.description: A comma separated list of the properties to be returned along with their history of previous values. If any of the specified properties are not present on the requested object(s), they will be ignored. Usage of this parameter will reduce the maximum number of objects that can be read by a single request

parameters.name: associations
parameters.description: A comma separated list of object types to retrieve associated IDs for. If any of the specified associations do not exist, they will be ignored

parameters.name: archived
parameters.description: Whether to return only results that have been archived

post.description: Create a quote with the given properties and return a copy of the object, including the ID. Documentation and examples for creating standard quotes is provided.
Create or update records identified by a unique property value as specified by the `idProperty` query param. `idProperty` query param refers to a property whose values are unique for the object.
Returns a paginated list of quote objects matching the specified search criteria, including property values and metadata.

links.description: Map of relevant links associated with the error response.
